children.heading: Institutional and program report cards on the quality of teacher preparation
children.label: a Institutional and program report cards on the quality of teacher preparation
I
children.text: whether the goals set under have been met; andsection 1022e of this title

children.label: II
children.text: a description of the activities the institution implemented to achieve such goals.

children.label: ii
children.text: A description of the steps the institution is taking to improve its performance in meeting the annual goals set under .section 1022e of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: A description of the activities the institution has implemented to meet the assurances provided under .section 1022e of this title

children.heading: Pass rates and scaled scores
children.label: B Pass rates and scaled scores
i
children.text: the percentage of students who have completed 100 percent of the nonclinical coursework and taken the assessment who pass such assessment;

children.label: ii
children.text: the percentage of all students who passed such assessment;

children.label: iii
children.text: the percentage of students who have taken such assessment who enrolled in and completed the traditional teacher preparation program or alternative routes to State certification or licensure program, as applicable;

children.label: iv
children.text: the average scaled score for all students who took such assessment;

children.label: v
children.text: a comparison of the program’s pass rates with the average pass rates for programs in the State; and

children.label: vi
children.text: a comparison of the program’s average scaled scores with the average scaled scores for programs in the State.

children.label: i
children.text: the criteria for admission into the program;

children.label: ii
children.text: the number of students in the program (disaggregated by race, ethnicity, and gender);

children.label: iii
children.text: the average number of hours of supervised clinical experience required for those in the program;

children.label: iv
children.text: the number of full-time equivalent faculty and students in the supervised clinical experience; and

children.label: v
children.text: the total number of students who have been certified or licensed as teachers, disaggregated by subject and area of certification or licensure.

children.heading: Statement
children.label: D Statement
children.text: In States that require approval or accreditation of teacher preparation programs, a statement of whether the institution’s program is so approved or accredited, and by whom.

children.heading: Designation as low-performing
children.label: E Designation as low-performing
children.text: Whether the program has been designated as low-performing by the State under .section 1022f(a) of this title

children.heading: Use of technology
children.label: F Use of technology
children.text: A description of the activities, including activities consistent with the principles of universal design for learning, that prepare teachers to integrate technology effectively into curricula and instruction, and to use technology effectively to collect, manage, and analyze data in order to improve teaching and learning for the purpose of increasing student academic achievement.

children.heading: Teacher training
children.label: G Teacher training
children.text: A description of the activities that prepare general education and special education teachers to teach students with disabilities effectively, including training related to participation as a member of individualized education program teams, as defined in , and to effectively teach students who are limited English proficient.section 1414(d)(1)(B) of this title

children.heading: Report
children.label: 2 Report
children.text: Each eligible partnership receiving a grant under shall report annually on the progress of the eligible partnership toward meeting the purposes of this part and the objectives and measures described in .section 1022a of this titlesection 1022c(a) of this title

children.heading: Fines
children.label: 3 Fines
children.text: The Secretary may impose a fine not to exceed $27,500 on an institution of higher education for failure to provide the information described in this subsection in a timely or accurate manner.

children.heading: Special rule
children.label: 4 Special rule
children.text: In the case of an institution of higher education that conducts a traditional teacher preparation program or alternative routes to State certification or licensure program and has fewer than 10 scores reported on any single initial teacher certification or licensure assessment during an academic year, the institution shall collect and publish information, as required under paragraph (1)(B), with respect to an average pass rate and scaled score on each State certification or licensure assessment taken over a three-year period.

children.heading: State report card on the quality of teacher preparation
children.label: b State report card on the quality of teacher preparation
A
children.text: A description of the reliability and validity of the teacher certification and licensure assessments, and any other certification and licensure requirements, used by the State.

children.label: B
children.text: The standards and criteria that prospective teachers must meet to attain initial teacher certification or licensure and to be certified or licensed to teach particular academic subjects, areas, or grades within the State.

children.label: C
children.text: A description of how the assessments and requirements described in subparagraph (A) are aligned with the challenging State academic standards required under and, as applicable, State early learning standards for early childhood education programs.section 6311(b)(1) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: for each institution of higher education located in the State and each entity located in the State, including those that offer an alternative route for teacher certification or licensure, the percentage of students at such institution or entity who have completed 100 percent of the nonclinical coursework and taken the assessment who pass such assessment;

children.label: ii
children.text: the percentage of all such students at all such institutions and entities who have taken the assessment who pass such assessment;

children.label: iii
children.text: the percentage of students who have taken the assessment who enrolled in and completed a teacher preparation program; and

children.label: iv
children.text: the average scaled score of individuals participating in such a program, or who have completed such a program during the two-year period preceding the first year for which the annual State report card is provided, who took each such assessment.

children.label: i
children.text: the percentage of individuals participating in such routes, or who have completed such routes during the two-year period preceding the date for which the determination is made, who passed each such assessment; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the average scaled score of individuals participating in such routes, or who have completed such routes during the two-year period preceding the first year for which the annual State report card is provided, who took each such assessment.

children.label: F
children.text: A description of the State’s criteria for assessing the performance of teacher preparation programs within institutions of higher education in the State. Such criteria shall include indicators of the academic content knowledge and teaching skills of students enrolled in such programs.

children.label: i
children.text: the criteria for admission into the program;

children.label: ii
children.text: the number of students in the program, disaggregated by race, ethnicity, and gender (except that such disaggregation shall not be required in a case in which the number of students in a category is insufficient to yield statistically reliable information or the results would reveal personally identifiable information about an individual student);

children.label: iii
children.text: the average number of hours of supervised clinical experience required for those in the program; and

children.label: iv
children.text: the number of full-time equivalent faculty, adjunct faculty, and students in supervised clinical experience.

children.label: i
children.text: area of certification or licensure;

children.label: ii
children.text: academic major; and

children.label: iii
children.text: subject area for which the teacher has been prepared to teach.

children.label: I
children.text: A description of the extent to which teacher preparation programs are addressing shortages of teachers who meet the applicable State certification and licensure requirements, including any requirements for certification obtained through alternative routes to certification, or, with regard to special education teachers, the qualifications described in , by area of certification or licensure, subject, and specialty, in the State’s public schools.section 1412(a)(14)(C) of this title

children.label: J
children.text: The extent to which teacher preparation programs prepare teachers, including general education and special education teachers, to teach students with disabilities effectively, including training related to participation as a member of individualized education program teams, as defined in .section 1414(d)(1)(B) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: integrate technology effectively into curricula and instruction, including activities consistent with the principles of universal design for learning; and

children.label: ii
children.text: use technology effectively to collect, manage, and analyze data to improve teaching and learning for the purpose of increasing student academic achievement.

children.label: L
children.text: The extent to which teacher preparation programs prepare teachers, including general education and special education teachers, to effectively teach students who are limited English proficient.

children.heading: Prohibition against creating a national list
children.label: 2 Prohibition against creating a national list
children.text: The Secretary shall not create a national list or ranking of States, institutions, or schools using the scaled scores provided under this subsection.

children.heading: Data quality
children.label: c Data quality
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe regulations to ensure the reliability, validity, integrity, and accuracy of the data submitted pursuant to this section.

children.heading: Report of the Secretary on the quality of teacher preparation
children.label: d Report of the Secretary on the quality of teacher preparation

children.heading: Report card
children.label: 1 Report card
children.text: The Secretary shall annually provide to the authorizing committees, and publish and make widely available, a report card on teacher qualifications and preparation in the United States, including all the information reported in subparagraphs (A) through (L) of subsection (b)(1). Such report shall identify States for which eligible partnerships received a grant under this part.

children.label: A
children.text: A comparison of States’ efforts to improve the quality of the current and future teaching force.

children.label: B
children.text: A comparison of eligible partnerships’ efforts to improve the quality of the current and future teaching force.

children.label: C
children.text: The national mean and median scaled scores and pass rate on any standardized test that is used in more than one State for teacher certification or licensure.

children.heading: Special rule
children.label: 3 Special rule
children.text: In the case of a teacher preparation program with fewer than ten scores reported on any single initial teacher certification or licensure assessment during an academic year, the Secretary shall collect and publish, and make publicly available, information with respect to an average pass rate and scaled score on each State certification or licensure assessment taken over a three-year period.

children.heading: Coordination
children.label: e Coordination
children.text: The Secretary, to the extent practicable, shall coordinate the information collected and published under this part among States for individuals who took State teacher certification or licensure assessments in a State other than the State in which the individual received the individual’s most recent degree.
